mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show negative mean or Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE increases exceed 0.5pp, or signal loses predictive power with p-value above 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance or Rank IC falls below 0.04 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals expanding manufacturing output that improves near-term earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes yield negative or flat 5-day forward returns during early-cycle phases, or the signal Sharpe ratio drops below 0.5 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of persistently low federal funds rates and rising unemployment creates a liquidity trap environment where monetary easing fails to stimulate growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors price in stagflation risks.
failure_condition: The joint condition produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% confirms recessionary conditions that force equity valuation multiples to contract, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth fails to predict negative 20-day returns with more than 50% accuracy, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across all recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels erode real corporate profit margins and prompt expectations of tighter monetary policy, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Increasing CPIAUCSL correlates with positive 60-day returns or shows no statistical relationship with p-value above 0.05 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly nonfarm payroll additions exceeding 200,000 signal robust economic activity that supports corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll increases above 200k yield negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below chance in walk-forward evaluation.
